 {"id":"bd-3ky.12","title":"Implement optional render/output thread (Mode B)","description":"# Implement optional render/output thread (Mode B)

## Background
Plan Sections 0.9.2 (Mode B) and 8.2 describe an optional dedicated render/output thread.

This exists to solve the real harness problem:
- logs can be produced by background tasks / subprocess capture
- UI needs tick-driven updates
- if output interleaves, inline mode breaks

Mode B keeps the **one-writer rule** by construction: the render thread is the only place bytes hit the terminal.

## Scope
- Feature-gated "Mode B" operation in addition to the default single-threaded mode.
- Render/output thread owns:
  - `TerminalSession` output handle
  - `Presenter`
  - `prev_buffer` (front buffer)
  - inline-mode policy state (ui rect/anchor/height)

## Design
### Messages
```rust
enum OutMsg {
    Log(Vec<u8>),
    Render(Buffer),
    Resize { w: u16, h: u16 },
    SetMode(ScreenMode),
    Shutdown,
}
```

### Coalescing rules
- Render messages are coalesced ("latest wins"):
  - drop intermediate renders if a newer one arrives
  - present at most one buffer per loop iteration
- Log messages are *not* dropped, but are:
  - newline-aware
  - chunked to avoid starving UI

### Error handling
- Render thread errors propagate back to the Program so we can:
  - restore terminal state
  - exit with a useful error

## Test Plan
- PTY tests must run in both modes:
  - default single-threaded mode
  - render/output thread enabled
- Stress tests:
  - heavy log spam + UI tick (spinner) + resize storms
  - ensure cursor invariants and scrollback preservation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mode B can be enabled via feature flag and/or App/Program option.
- [ ] All terminal writes are serialized in the render thread.
- [ ] Render coalescing works (latest buffer presented).
- [ ] Log chunking works and does not starve UI.
- [ ] PTY tests pass in both Mode A and Mode B.

## Location
Likely `ftui-core` (thread + terminal ownership) with integration hooks in `ftui-runtime`.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Background thread for frame rendering
- [ ] Double buffering with atomic swap
- [ ] Frame rate limiting (configurable FPS cap)
- [ ] Frame skipping when behind schedule
- [ ] Graceful shutdown without hanging
- [ ] Error propagation to main thread
- [ ] Submit frame (non-blocking)
- [ ] Flush pending frames (blocking)

### Threading Requirements
- [ ] Main thread never blocked on I/O
- [ ] Channel-based command passing
- [ ] No data races on buffer swap
- [ ] Proper synchronization on shutdown

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Submit latency: < 100µs
- [ ] Render latency: < 1ms for 80x24
- [ ] Memory: 2 buffers + channel overhead
- [ ] No allocation per frame (buffer reuse)

### Error Handling
- [ ] I/O errors propagated to main thread
- [ ] Panics in render thread caught
- [ ] Graceful degradation on slow I/O

- [ ] tracing::debug! for frame submission
- [ ] tracing::debug! for frame skip
- [ ] tracing::trace! for render timing
- [ ] tracing::error! for I/O failures
- [ ] tracing::info! for shutdown

- [ ] Unit test: submit/flush cycle
- [ ] Unit test: frame rate limiting
- [ ] Stress test: high FPS submission
- [ ] Stress test: large frame buffers

 {"id":"bd-3ky.13","title":"Implement language-specific tokenizers (Rust, Python, JS, JSON, TOML, Markdown)","description":"# Implement language tokenizers (optional)

## Objective
Provide per-language token definitions/adapters for the syntax tokenizer engine so we can highlight common formats.

Phase 7 extra; feature-gated.

## Scope
- Languages/formats (initial set):
  - Rust
  - Python
  - JavaScript/TypeScript
  - JSON
  - TOML
  - Markdown (for fenced code blocks)

## Design Notes
- Keep correctness and safety first: token ranges must always be valid.
- Avoid heavyweight parsing; tokenization is for highlighting, not compilation.

## Tests Required
- Table-driven tests per language with representative snippets.
- Ensure:
  - keywords/strings/comments detected
  - token ranges valid
  - no panics on malformed input

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one language tokenizer works end-to-end in `bd-381`.
- [ ] Each supported language has tests with representative fixtures.
- [ ] Malformed input never panics and produces valid ranges.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:47.816763643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:10:29.814139632Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:10:29.814073037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.13","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:59:53.710696913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.13","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:56.197642632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3ky.13","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ky.14","title":"Implement text View (scrollable text display)","description":"# Implement Text View (scrolling + wrapping) (optional)

## Objective
Implement a text “view” utility that can:
- compute visible ranges given scroll offset
- perform wrapping (word-wrap + fallback)
- support gutters/line numbers (optional)

This is a Phase 7 extra, but it can be leveraged by the harness Viewport/LogViewer.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/opentui_rust/src/text/view.rs`

## Scope
- Viewport math:
  - scroll to line
  - scroll by page
  - compute visible lines/range
- Wrapping:
  - word wrap
  - character wrap fallback
- Optional:
  - line number gutter
  - selection highlights

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for visible range calculations.
- Snapshot tests for wrapping behavior with Unicode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] View calculations are deterministic and tested.
- [ ] Wrapping is Unicode-width correct (uses ftui width rules).
- [ ] Viewport/LogViewer uses this (no duplicate wrapping/scroll logic).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseBrook","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:54.331187709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:16:04.603634190Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:16:04.603614573Z","close_reason":"Implemented TextView with wrapping + viewport math + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:14:44.620336451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:57.288139223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:59:54.357047639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:56.744682359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:57.928832265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-3ky.14","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ky.15","title":"OpenTUI Core Systems Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for OpenTUI core systems. These are foundational components that require rigorous testing including fuzzing, property tests, and performance benchmarks.

## Testing Philosophy
Core systems require:
1. **Correctness** - Behavior matches specification exactly
2. **Robustness** - Handles malformed input gracefully
3. **Performance** - Meets latency and throughput targets
4. **Security** - No buffer overflows, escape injection, etc.

## Test Requirements by Module

### ANSI Sequence Generator (bd-3ky.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_sgr_reset` - \x1b[0m emitted correctly
- `test_sgr_fg_16` - ANSI 16-color foreground
- `test_sgr_fg_256` - 256-color foreground
- `test_sgr_fg_rgb` - 24-bit foreground
- `test_sgr_bg_all` - Background color variants
- `test_sgr_bold` - Bold attribute
- `test_sgr_italic` - Italic attribute
- `test_sgr_underline_variants` - Single, double, curly
- `test_cursor_absolute` - CUP sequence
- `test_cursor_relative` - CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB
- `test_cursor_save_restore` - DECSC/DECRC
- `test_erase_line` - EL variants
- `test_erase_screen` - ED variants
- `test_scroll_region` - DECSTBM
- `test_mouse_enable` - SGR mouse mode
- `test_bracketed_paste` - Enable/disable
- `test_sync_output` - DEC mode 2026
- `test_osc_hyperlink` - OSC 8 link sequences

Property tests:
- `prop_sequences_valid_ascii` - All bytes are valid escape sequences
- `prop_sequences_terminate` - All sequences properly terminated

Conformance tests:
- `conformance_vt100` - VT100 compatibility
- `conformance_xterm` - xterm extensions

### Buffer Drawing (bd-3ky.2)
Unit tests:
- `test_draw_hline` - Horizontal line placement
- `test_draw_vline` - Vertical line placement
- `test_draw_line_diagonal` - Bresenham algorithm
- `test_draw_rect_outline` - Rectangle border only
- `test_draw_rect_filled` - Filled rectangle
- `test_draw_border` - Border with corner chars
- `test_print_ascii` - ASCII text at position
- `test_print_unicode` - Wide characters handled
- `test_print_styled` - Style applied to cells
- `test_blit_opaque` - Buffer copied without alpha
- `test_blit_alpha` - Alpha blending during blit
- `test_scissor_clips` - Drawing clipped to scissor rect

Property tests:
- `prop_draw_in_bounds` - All draws stay within buffer
- `prop_scissor_respected` - Nothing drawn outside scissor

Benchmark tests:
- `bench_draw_line_1000` - 1000 lines < 1ms
- `bench_blit_full_screen` - 80x24 blit < 100µs

### Syntax Tokenizer (bd-3ky.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_tokenize_empty` - Empty string yields no tokens
- `test_tokenize_whitespace` - Whitespace token type
- `test_tokenize_keyword` - Language keywords detected
- `test_tokenize_string_single` - Single-quoted strings
- `test_tokenize_string_double` - Double-quoted strings
- `test_tokenize_string_escape` - Escaped characters in strings
- `test_tokenize_string_multiline` - State preserved across lines
- `test_tokenize_comment_line` - // and # comments
- `test_tokenize_comment_block` - /* */ comments
- `test_tokenize_number_int` - Integer literals
- `test_tokenize_number_float` - Float literals
- `test_tokenize_number_hex` - Hexadecimal
- `test_tokenize_operator` - Operators detected
- `test_tokenize_incremental` - Re-tokenize after edit

Fuzz tests:
- `fuzz_tokenizer_no_panic` - Random input never panics
- `fuzz_tokenizer_valid_ranges` - Token ranges always valid

Performance tests:
- `perf_tokenize_10k_lines` - 10,000 lines < 100ms

### Highlight Theme (bd-3ky.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_theme_load_toml` - Parse theme from TOML
- `test_theme_load_json` - Parse theme from JSON
- `test_theme_builtin_dark` - Dark theme colors
- `test_theme_builtin_light` - Light theme colors
- `test_theme_token_style` - Style lookup by token type
- `test_theme_fallback` - Unknown token uses default
- `test_highlighted_buffer_cache` - Cache invalidation on edit

### Text Rope (bd-3ky.5)
Unit tests:
- `test_rope_from_empty` - Empty rope
- `test_rope_from_string` - Initialize from string
- `test_rope_len_bytes` - Byte length tracking
- `test_rope_len_lines` - Line count tracking
- `test_rope_insert_start` - Insert at position 0
- `test_rope_insert_middle` - Insert in middle
- `test_rope_insert_end` - Append
- `test_rope_delete_range` - Delete byte range
- `test_rope_replace` - Delete + insert
- `test_rope_line_access` - Get line by index
- `test_rope_slice` - Extract substring
- `test_rope_byte_to_line_col` - Coordinate conversion
- `test_rope_line_col_to_byte` - Reverse conversion

Property tests:
- `prop_rope_insert_delete_inverse` - Insert then delete = original
- `prop_rope_len_consistent` - len() matches actual content
- `prop_rope_lines_consistent` - line count matches newlines + 1

Performance tests:
- `perf_rope_insert_random` - 10,000 random inserts < 100ms
- `perf_rope_large_file` - 1MB file operations < 10ms

### Text Editor (bd-3ky.6)
Unit tests:
- `test_editor_insert_char` - Single character insertion
- `test_editor_insert_text` - Multi-char insertion
- `test_editor_delete` - Delete forward
- `test_editor_backspace` - Delete backward
- `test_editor_move_cursor` - Arrow key movement
- `test_editor_move_word` - Ctrl+Arrow movement
- `test_editor_select_shift` - Shift+Arrow selection
- `test_editor_select_all` - Ctrl+A selection
- `test_editor_cut_copy_paste` - Clipboard operations
- `test_editor_undo` - Single undo
- `test_editor_undo_chain` - Multiple undos
- `test_editor_redo` - Redo after undo
- `test_editor_redo_discard` - New edit clears redo stack
- `test_editor_transaction` - Grouped operations

Property tests:
- `prop_editor_cursor_valid` - Cursor always in bounds
- `prop_editor_undo_redo_inverse` - Full undo/redo is identity

### Unicode BiDi (bd-3ky.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_bidi_ltr_only` - Left-to-right text unchanged
- `test_bidi_rtl_only` - Right-to-left reversed
- `test_bidi_mixed` - LTR with embedded RTL
- `test_bidi_numbers_in_rtl` - Numbers in Arabic text
- `test_bidi_explicit_marks` - LRM/RLM characters
- `test_bidi_reorder_for_display` - Visual reordering

Conformance tests:
- `conformance_uax9` - Unicode BiDi algorithm tests

### Unicode Normalization (bd-3ky.8)
Unit tests:
- `test_nfc_combining` - Combining characters composed
- `test_nfd_decompose` - Precomposed decomposed
- `test_nfkc_compatibility` - Compatibility mappings
- `test_nfkd_full_decompose` - Full compatibility decomposition
- `test_quick_check_fast` - Already-normalized detected quickly

Conformance tests:
- `conformance_normalization_tests` - Unicode consortium test suite

### Unicode Search (bd-3ky.9)
Unit tests:
- `test_search_exact` - Exact substring match
- `test_search_case_insensitive` - Case-folded match
- `test_search_normalized` - NFC-normalized match
- `test_search_whole_word` - Word boundary detection
- `test_search_regex` - Regex pattern matching
- `test_search_find_all` - All occurrences returned
- `test_search_find_next` - Forward iteration
- `test_search_find_prev` - Backward iteration

### Terminal Raw Mode (bd-3ky.10)
Unit tests:
- `test_raw_mode_enter` - Mode entered successfully
- `test_raw_mode_exit` - Mode restored on drop
- `test_raw_mode_panic_cleanup` - Cleaned up on panic
- `test_alt_screen_enter` - Switch to alt screen
- `test_alt_screen_exit` - Return to main screen
- `test_mouse_mode_enable` - Mouse events received
- `test_signal_handler` - SIGWINCH handled

PTY tests:
- `pty_raw_mode_isolation` - Mode changes isolated to session
- `pty_cleanup_on_crash` - Terminal restored after kill -9

### Terminal Queries (bd-3ky.11)
Unit tests:
- `test_query_cursor_position` - CPR response parsed
- `test_query_terminal_size` - Size query works
- `test_query_timeout` - Timeout on no response
- `test_query_partial_response` - Handle incomplete response

### Threaded Renderer (bd-3ky.12)
Unit tests:
- `test_renderer_submit_frame` - Frame accepted
- `test_renderer_frame_rate_limit` - FPS capped
- `test_renderer_skip_frames` - Old frames skipped when behind
- `test_renderer_shutdown_clean` - Graceful shutdown
- `test_renderer_error_propagation` - I/O errors reach main thread

Stress tests:
- `stress_renderer_high_fps` - 1000 FPS submission
- `stress_renderer_large_frames` - 200x50 terminal

### Language Tokenizers (bd-3ky.13)
Unit tests per language:
- Rust: keywords, lifetimes, macros, attributes, strings, comments
- Python: keywords, decorators, f-strings, comments, indentation
- JavaScript: keywords, template literals, JSX, comments
- JSON: keys, strings, numbers, booleans, null, structure
- TOML: sections, keys, strings, arrays, inline tables
- Markdown: headers, emphasis, links, code blocks, lists

### Text View (bd-3ky.14)
Unit tests:
- `test_view_scroll_to_line` - Scroll to specific line
- `test_view_scroll_by_page` - Page up/down
- `test_view_visible_range` - Correct range calculation
- `test_view_wrap_word` - Word wrap at boundaries
- `test_view_wrap_char` - Character wrap fallback
- `test_view_line_numbers` - Line number gutter
- `test_view_selection_highlight` - Selection styling
- `test_view_search_highlight` - Search result styling

## Logging Requirements
- `tracing::debug!` for state transitions
- `tracing::trace!` for per-operation details
- `tracing::warn!` for recovered errors
- `tracing::error!` for fatal conditions
- Performance-sensitive paths use `tracing::trace_span!`

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 95% (core systems are critical)
- Branch coverage: >= 90%
- Fuzzing: 1M iterations without crash

Dependencies: All OpenTUI modules, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test scope is aligned to the ftui components we actually ship (kernel-critical vs optional extras are clearly separated).
- [ ] Suite includes unit + property + snapshot and PTY integration tests where appropriate.
- [ ] Failures produce actionable logs (bytes/model diffs) and are reproducible in  {"id":"bd-3ky.2","title":"Implement Buffer drawing operations","description":"# Implement Buffer drawing operations

## Objective
Add ergonomic, well-tested drawing helpers on top of the core `Buffer` so widgets/renderables can be implemented without duplicating low-level cell loops.

## Why This Exists
The kernel is intentionally minimal, but widgets still need:
- borders/lines
- filling rects
- blitting
- text printing respecting clipping

If each widget reimplements these loops, we’ll get inconsistent behavior and bugs.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/opentui_rust/src/` buffer/drawing modules

## Scope
Implement a `BufferDraw`-style API (name TBD) that operates on a `Buffer` and respects:
- scissor stack (no drawing outside clip)
- opacity stack (alpha compositing rules)

Operations to support (minimum):
- draw horizontal/vertical lines
- draw rectangle outline + filled rectangle
- draw borders using a provided border character set
- print text at position (ASCII + Unicode) without writing out-of-bounds
- blit/copy regions between buffers (opaque + alpha)

Optional (Phase 7 / nice-to-have):
- Bresenham diagonal line
- scroll-region related helpers for inline mode

## Design Notes
- Keep cell loops cache-friendly (row-major).
- Avoid allocations inside tight loops.
- All drawing must be clipped via scissor (even if caller passes a bad rect).

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for each operation with small fixed buffers (readable expected grids).
- Property tests:
  - never writes out of bounds
  - scissor is always respected
  - opacity stack always clamps to [0,1]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API exists for the “minimum” scope operations above.
- [ ] All operations respect scissor and opacity stacks.
- [ ] Unit tests cover border/rect/text/blit semantics.
- [ ] At least one widget/renderable depends on this (no dead code).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:01:07.671267058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:42:55.020031764Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:42:55.020010684Z","close_reason":"Implemented enhanced Buffer drawing operations: BorderChars struct with 5 presets (SQUARE, ROUNDED, DOUBLE, HEAVY, ASCII), print_text returns u16, print_text_clipped with max_x, draw_border, draw_box, paint_area.  {"id":"bd-3ky.3","title":"Implement syntax tokenizer engine","description":"# Implement syntax tokenizer engine (optional)

## Objective
Implement a tokenization engine that can turn source text into a stream of tokens suitable for syntax highlighting.

  - token kind
  - byte range (start..end)
  - optional metadata (e.g., nesting)

- Unit tests for basic token kinds (keywords/strings/comments) on small fixtures.
- Fuzz tests ensuring no panics and valid ranges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tokenizer engine API exists and is used by syntax highlighting (`bd-381`).
- [ ] Tokens always have valid, in-bounds ranges.
- [ ] Unit tests + fuzz/no-panic tests exist.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New languages can be added without code changes
- [ ] Fallback to plain text for unknown languages

### Performance Requirements

- [ ] Tokenize 10,000 lines: < 100ms
- [ ] Incremental update: < 1ms for single line change
- [ ] Memory: < 50 bytes per line state

- [ ] tracing::warn! for tokenization errors

 {"id":"bd-6e9.7","title":"Implement Cursor utilities module","description":"# Implement cursor utilities for text editing widgets

## Objective
Provide cursor movement and coordinate conversion helpers for text editing widgets (TextInput/TextArea) that are:
- Unicode-aware (graphemes + display width)
- deterministic
- reusable across widgets and editor core

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/crates/bubbles/src/cursor.rs`

## Scope
- A `CursorPosition` type that tracks:
  - logical position (line + byte offset, or grapheme index)
  - visual column (display cell column)
- Movement helpers:
  - left/right by grapheme
  - up/down by line
  - word-left/word-right (define word boundaries explicitly)
  - start/end of line
  - start/end of document (optional)
- Mapping between:
  - (row, visual_col) ↔ logical offsets

## Design Notes
- Do not depend on Buffer/GraphemePool internals; cursor utils operate on text, not rendered cells.
- Visual column must use the same width rules as the rendering pipeline.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests covering:
  - ASCII fast path
  - combining marks
  - emoji + ZWJ sequences
  - tabs (if supported; define tab width)
  - word boundary behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-6e9.8","title":"Implement grapheme segmentation helpers for ftui-text","description":"# Implement grapheme segmentation helpers for ftui-text

## Background
Plan requirements:
- width correctness for emoji/ZWJ/combining marks is a stop-ship gate
- ftui-text needs grapheme segmentation helpers for wrapping/truncation/alignment

We should isolate Unicode segmentation logic behind a small set of helpers so:
- widgets/text algorithms stay readable
- we can swap implementation details without touching call sites

## Scope
Provide utilities built on `unicode-segmentation` + `unicode-width`:
- grapheme iteration
- grapheme-aware truncation to display width
- (optional) word boundaries / soft wrap points

Example APIs (shape, not necessarily final):
```rust
pub fn grapheme_count(s: &str) -> usize;
pub fn graphemes(s: &str) -> impl Iterator<Item = &str>;
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize; // uses ASCII fast path where applicable (bd-v6y)

/// Returns (prefix, width) where prefix fits within max_width.
pub fn truncate_to_width(s: &str, max_width: usize) -> (&str, usize);
```

## Non-goals
- Full Unicode normalization/bidi/search subsystems (out of scope for plan v1)

## Tests
- Grapheme iteration matches expectations on:
  - ASCII
  - combining marks
  - emoji + ZWJ sequences
- truncate_to_width never splits mid-grapheme

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Helpers exist and are used by Text wrapping/truncation code.
- [ ] Unicode width corpus tests (bd-16k) can exercise these helpers.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-7u8","title":"Implement cursor save/restore strategy (DEC vs ANSI vs emulated)","description":"# Implement Cursor Save/Restore Strategy

## Background
Plan Section 9.3 describes a critical cursor save/restore strategy for inline mode robustness. Terminals differ in which save/restore sequences they support reliably.

## Problem Statement
Inline mode requires saving cursor position before drawing UI and restoring after. However:
- DEC save/restore (\`ESC 7\` / \`ESC 8\`) is preferred but not universal
- ANSI save/restore (\`CSI s\` / \`CSI u\`) is an alternative
- Some terminals/muxes have quirks with either
- Must handle failures gracefully

## Layered Strategy (from Plan)

### Layer 1: DEC Save/Restore (Preferred)
\`\`\`rust
const DEC_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b7";    // ESC 7 (DECSC)
const DEC_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b8"; // ESC 8 (DECRC)
\`\`\`
- Most widely supported on modern terminals
- Saves more state (cursor position, attributes, charset)
- Works in tmux/screen with passthrough

### Layer 2: ANSI Save/Restore (Fallback)
\`\`\`rust
const ANSI_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[s";    // CSI s
const ANSI_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[u"; // CSI u
\`\`\`
- Less widely supported
- Only saves cursor position (not attributes)
- May conflict with some terminal modes

### Layer 3: Emulated (Last Resort)
\`\`\`rust
// Track cursor position explicitly
struct CursorTracker {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
}

impl CursorTracker {
    fn save(&mut self, current_x: u16, current_y: u16) {
        self.x = current_x;
        self.y = current_y;
    }
    
    fn restore(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", self.y + 1, self.x + 1)
    }
}
\`\`\`
- Works everywhere that supports CUP
- Requires tracking cursor position throughout
- More overhead but guaranteed to work

## Strategy Selection
\`\`\`rust
pub enum CursorSaveStrategy {
    Dec,       // ESC 7 / ESC 8
    Ansi,      // CSI s / CSI u
    Emulated,  // Track and CUP
    Auto,      // Detect best for environment
}

impl CursorSaveStrategy {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Most terminals support DEC
        if !caps.in_screen {
            return Self::Dec;
        }
        // Screen has quirks, try ANSI
        Self::Ansi
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
\`\`\`rust
pub struct CursorManager {
    strategy: CursorSaveStrategy,
    saved_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,  // For emulated mode
}

impl CursorManager {
    pub fn save(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write, current_pos: (u16, u16)) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                self.saved_position = Some(current_pos);
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => {
                // Try DEC, it's most common
                writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE)
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn restore(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                if let Some((x, y)) = self.saved_position {
                    write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", y + 1, x + 1)?;
                }
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Background & Motivation

Layout bugs are notoriously hard to debug:
- Constraints are implicit in code
- Computed values aren't visible
- Overflows and underflows silent

**The solution:**
- Visual debugger for layout constraints
- Shows requested vs received sizes
- Highlights overflow/underflow in red
- Tree view of layout hierarchy

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-3ky.4 (highlight theme system)
- Requires bd-yzu (Flex layout with constraint solving)
- Complements bd-17nn (Widget Introspection Debug Overlay)

## Technical Design

```rust

    enabled: bool,

}

    area_received: Rect,
    constraints: LayoutConstraints,
    children: Vec<LayoutRecord>,
}

pub struct LayoutConstraints {
    min_width: u16,
    max_width: u16,
    min_height: u16,
    max_height: u16,
}

impl LayoutDebugger {
    pub fn record(&mut self, name: &str, requested: Rect, received: Rect, constraints: LayoutConstraints) { ... }

}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Constraint violation (WARN level)
tracing::warn!(
    widget = name,
    requested = ?requested,
    received = ?received,
    "Layout constraint violation"
);

// Layout computed (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(
    widget = name,
    constraints = ?constraints,
    result = ?area,
    "Layout computed"
);
```
